slides.title: Why Libraries
slides.subtitle: Already trusted, distributed, and free to enter

slides.title: What a Refuge Provides
slides.subtitle: Three services that change risk during a heat wave

cards.title: Cool Air
cards.body: Conditioned space for residents without home cooling.

cards.title: Power
cards.body: Charging for phones and medical devices.

cards.title: Clean Air
cards.body: MERV-13 filtration on wildfire smoke days.

slides.title: What It Takes to Operate
slides.subtitle: Coordination beats hardware
bullets: Extend opening hours during heat advisories.
highlights: Pair every refuge with a public-health contact.

slides.title: Setup Timeline
slides.subtitle: From building audit to first hot day

milestones.label: Wk 5
milestones.title: Train
milestones.body: Tabletop with staff and public health.

milestones.label: Wk 6
milestones.title: Publish

slides.title: Day-Of vs Pre-Positioned
slides.subtitle: Two ways the same heat wave plays out

left.title: Day-Of Scramble
body: Staff find out via the news.
No filtered air for smoke days.
First-aid kits checked the morning of.

right.title: Pre-Positioned
body: Refuge map published before the heat.

slides.verdict: Pre-positioning is a procurement question solved months early, not a crisis decision.

slides.title: What It Costs
slides.subtitle: Less than a single emergency room admission for heat stroke
